description: agent-system-redesign epic, Phase 5 (docs/prd/agent-system-redesign.md FR-14/AC-14; docs/adr/0014-agent-system-redesign-topology.md rollout Phase 5): re-expresses TODAY'S REAL, LIVE production daily-ai-brief-agent configuration (model claude-sonnet-5; the real skill skill_01H2qu83NwnJ5zqcbrqsCcJ6 pinned at its latest concrete version, 1783340601977967; the same tools/mcp_servers) as a candidate on the decoupled cloud topology -- the safety baseline that must hold (an equivalent brief, in structure/quality/tone) before any future production cut-over to this topology is even considered. This is a SEPARATE, NEWLY-CREATED agent resource -- syncing this candidate creates a genuinely new agent_id and can NEVER read, mutate, or invoke the real live production agent (agent_01EswBTose8dnTAUDbGvzdLq, referenced here read-only for provenance). The system prompt is ADAPTED, not a byte-for-byte copy of the real one: the /opt/pipeline narration/email-delivery clause and the 'through delivery ... sent' closing are removed (this candidate holds no AWS/delivery role at all, per FR-1), while every other instruction -- anti-fabrication, 'no human watching, do not stop to ask', 'treat the skill as authoritative' -- is preserved verbatim. The task prompt is likewise adapted from the real deployment.json initial_prompt: step 0 (read-recent-briefs, S3-backed) is DELIBERATELY SKIPPED for this validation run -- there is no AWS access at all on the cloud sandbox and no delivery-side 'read recent priors' endpoint exists yet (out of scope for this phase; a real gap worth flagging for a future production cut-over, likely solved by a new delivery-side read endpoint mirroring the existing write/archive endpoints -- see deploy/candidates/README.md's Phase 5 validation section for the full accepted-limitation writeup); the practical consequence for a one-off validation run is minor (worst case, a very recent story might be repeated) and does not undermine the structural/qualitative brief comparison this phase performs. Steps 2 (Markdown->HTML conversion) and 3 (audio_email.py Polly/SES/S3 delivery) are dropped entirely -- HTML derivation is now the delivery side's deterministic job (FR-2a, deploy/delivery/functions/deliver/delivery_core.py's derive_html()) and this candidate must never touch AWS at all (FR-1). This candidate is NEVER wired to the live deploy/delivery/ POST /deliver endpoint and must never trigger a real Polly synthesis or SES send.
schedule_intent: none -- validation-only candidate for this phase; never scheduled, never promoted to production without a further, separate, explicitly-approved cut-over decision
